steps: Go to https://console.cloud.google.com and log in with your Google account
Create a new project or select an existing one
Go to APIs & Services > Library and search for 'Cloud Storage API', then click 'Enable'
Go to APIs & Services > Credentials at https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials
Configure the OAuth consent screen if prompted (set User Type to 'External', add app name and contact email)
Select 'Web application' as the application type, enter a name, and add your redirect URI
Click 'Create' and copy both the Client ID and Client Secret from the dialog

credential_fields.name: client_id
credential_fields.label: Client ID
credential_fields.description: OAuth 2.0 Client ID from Google Cloud Console

credential_fields.name: client_secret
credential_fields.label: Client Secret
credential_fields.description: OAuth 2.0 Client Secret from Google Cloud Console

notes: Cloud Storage API must be enabled in the API Library before use
The OAuth consent screen must be configured before credentials can be used
For testing, add your Google account as a test user under the OAuth consent screen
Client secret is shown only at creation time; download the JSON file from the credentials page to save it
For server-to-server access, consider using a Service Account with a JSON key instead of OAuth 2.0 client credentials
Google Cloud Storage pricing: free 5 GB storage and 1 GB network egress per month; standard storage is $0.020/GB/month
